feed.title: Ferbot's blog
feed.subtitle: My personal blog where I explore red team techniques

entry.title: Bastard from HackTheBox
entry.summary: HTB: Bastard What a bastard was this one. User shell was harder than I thought as ruby scripts didn’t work out for me and ended up using a python poc out there in the wild. PrivEsc was easy because of metasploit, I tried to escalate by running kernel exploits but couldn’t. Initial foothold Let’s start right away with an intense nmap scan. nmap -Pn -p- -sV --max-retries=1 --min-rate=1000 10....

entry.title: Optimum from HackTheBox
entry.summary: HTB: Optimum I have always thought Windows machines are harder than linux, at least when refering to privilege escalation. User shell was pretty straight forward as there was only one port and only one external service running. I was lucky as Windows privesc tends to be more difficult and got root as second try. Initial foothold An intense nmap scan to get a first hint on the machine. nmap ...
